mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high unemployment (UNRATE > 10%) signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment, or UNRATE fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 100.5) indicates expanding manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or 20-day returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1 > 1.5%) and industrial production (INDPRO > 100.5) signals robust economic expansion that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to predict returns (interaction term p > 0.1) or 60-day returns in co-expanding regimes are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In high-interest-rate environments (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) pressures central banks to tighten policy, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict returns (interaction term p > 0.05) or 5-day returns in high-rate environments are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 580k) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 355k) indicate elevated demand pressures that drive corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict returns (interaction term p > 0.1) or 20-day returns in high-demand periods are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Excess payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high payroll growth, or PAYEMS fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (GDPC1 > 1.5%), rising housing starts (HOUST > 580k) amplify demand-side momentum, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict returns (interaction term p > 0.05) or 60-day returns in expansionary regimes are not significantly positive.
